titleStmt.title: Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 605

p.seg: William by the grace of God king of the Scots to all worthy men of his entire land, clerk and lay, greeting.

Let present and future know me to have granted, and by this my charter to have made firm, the renunciation and abjuration that Walter of Gullane with Nicholas and John his brothers, and with Emma his maternal aunt, made for himself and his kindred in the full court of the prior of Coldingham, of the toun of greater Lumsdaine with everything belonging to it, and that which he had by right in the same toun, in return for one ploughgate of land which the prior of Durham granted to the said Walter and his heirs in the territory of Swinton. Wherefore I will and strictly enjoin that the said renunciation and abjuration be observed as firmly and inviolably as the charter of the said William bears witness. Saving my service.
As witnesses, Walter and William my chaplains, Philip de Valognes my chamberlain, William Comyn, William del Bois, and Hugh, my clerks.
At Haddington on the 8th day of July.
